bids.reasoning: At the start of the negotiation, I am targeting maximum utility because there is no time pressure to concede yet. With no opponent offers or messages, I have no evidence about their priorities, so I will open with my highest-value bid across all issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid, about 0.973 for me, because it is still extremely early at t=0.02 and there is no real time pressure to accept a worst-case counteroffer. The opponent’s message is a threat, but their only bid so far is all valueA, which is very poor for me and shows no concession yet. I will make a small concession on my lowest-weight issue, issueA, while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.947 because it is still very early at t=0.04 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueA offer, which is very poor for me and shows no real concession despite their pressure messages. I will concede only on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and D.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for myself while making a concession on my lowest-weight issue to keep bargaining moving. The opponent has repeatedly offered all valueA, which is only low utility for me, and their messages are threats/urgency not supported by the actual early time. I will concede issueA fully but hold firm on the higher-weight issues B, C, and D.

At t=0.08 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small additional concession beyond my last offer to avoid repeating it. The opponent has held firm at all valueA and used threats, but their bidding behavior shows no actual concession, so I will not accept their low-utility position and will only concede on the lower-weight issues first.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest additional concession to keep movement alive. The opponent has mostly held at valueA but recently improved issueD to valueB, so I treat the threats as cheap talk but acknowledge some concession signal; I concede further on lower-weight issueB while holding firm on high-weight issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.76, conceding further on the lower-weight issueB while holding firm on issueC and issueD, which matter most to me. Time is still early at t=0.12, so the opponent’s threats to walk are not credible enough to justify a major concession; their actual bids show only a small move from issueD:valueA to issueD:valueB while otherwise holding firm.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68: this concedes further from my last offer while still protecting issueD, my highest-weight issue. At t=0.14 it is still early, so I should not accept the opponent’s very low offers or their threats at face value, but their repeated bids suggest they are holding firm and only slightly moved on issueD. I will concede on issueC before issueD, since issueD matters most to me.

I am targeting about 0.60 utility: a meaningful concession from my last offer while still protecting issueD, my highest-weight issue. The opponent’s threats look like cheap talk at this early time, but their offers show slight movement on issueC and issueD, so I will respond by conceding issueC to valueB while holding issueD at valueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.57, conceding more on the lower-weight issues while still protecting value on the two most important issues. At t=0.18 it is still early enough not to accept the opponent’s very low-utility positions, and their repeated threats are cheap talk; their actual offers show only small movement, mainly around issueC/issueD. This bid avoids repeating my previous offer and tests a tradeoff by lowering issueD from my maximum while improving issueC.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a bid around 0.65 utility rather than accepting the opponent’s latest low-utility offer. The opponent has made only small concessions and their threats are not credible given the actual time remaining, but they have signaled some movement on issueB and issueC, so I concede there while holding firm on high-weight issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility of about 0.625 while making a visible concession on my most important issueD from valueD down to valueC. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly for me and their repeated threats are cheap talk at t=0.22, but they have signaled pressure around issueD, so I am testing a compromise there while keeping issueC relatively favorable.

I am targeting a utility around 0.57 by holding issueC and issueD at valueC while conceding the lower-weight issueA and issueB to valueA. At t=0.24 there is still time, so I should not accept their low-utility threat-heavy offers, but their latest move to issueD:valueC signals some willingness to move on my most important issue.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.49, which is a concession from my previous offer but still above the opponent’s latest bid utility to me. Time is still early-mid at t=0.26, so I should not collapse to their exact terms, but their offers show some movement on issueC and issueD, while their messages are mostly threats and not independently credible.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.47, conceding on lower-value issues and partially on issueD while preserving some value above the opponent’s current offer. At t=0.28 there is still time, so I should not collapse to their low-utility demand, but their bids show real concessions on issueC and occasional movement on issueD despite repeated threats. I therefore combine their recent signal of issueC=valueC with a compromise on issueD=valueB.

I am targeting a utility just under 0.50, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier offers but still protects my highest-weight issueD. At t=0.30 there is no need to accept the opponent’s very low latest bid; their threats are cheap talk, and their actual offers show only limited movement with occasional concessions on issueD or issueC. I will vary from my previous bid by trading down on issueC while improving issueD, keeping a plausible compromise above reservation value.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.55, since it is still early-mid negotiation and there is no need to drop to the opponent’s low offers yet. The opponent’s messages are mostly threats and urgency, but their actual bids show only limited concessions, especially around issueB and issueD, so I am incorporating their recent issueB movement while holding issueD at valueC due to its high importance.

I am targeting about 0.49 utility: a meaningful concession from my prior bid while still preserving value on issueD, my most important issue. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk, but their offers show limited movement and some willingness to give valueB on issueC and previously valueC on issueD, so I am combining those signals while conceding low-weight issues first.
